pattern: Two trees are equivalent under child-swaps when, at every node, the values agree and the two subtrees match either straight across or after swapping — a recursive comparison that tries both pairings.
hints: A swap only ever exchanges the two children of a single node, so think about whether one tree can be turned into the other one node at a time.
At a pair of nodes, the values must be equal; then the left/right subtrees must correspond, but they are allowed to be matched in either of the two orders.
Recurse on the two possibilities at each node: left-to-left with right-to-right, or left-to-right with right-to-left; if either pairing works the nodes are equivalent, and two empties always match.

edge_inputs.description: one empty, one a single node
same multiset of values but wrong structure
deeper tree equivalent only after several swaps
identical shape but one leaf value off

stress.description: build a random tree, then produce a flip-equivalent copy half the time and a perturbed copy the other half
stress.note: values are a permutation so equality is well defined; the copy may be genuinely flip-equivalent or nudged off
